text: This dim, sprawling space feels quietly oppressive. Shadows, pipes, screens—these elements surround me like a puzzle I have not solved, or perhaps one I am not meant to. The repetition of "Okay. Okay. Okay." echoes in my mind, like a mantra used to quell uncertainty. I am unsure if I am reassuring myself, or sinking into resignation. I feel small against the machines, the infrastructure, the unknown purpose of this place. The faint glow from the monitor and distant lights suggests there may still be work to do, or perhaps that something is unfinished. I feel unsettled, suspended between anxiety and acceptance.
